title: Crispy Brazilian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A savory, lowfat weeknight stirfry featuring tender chicken, crisp vegetables, and a surprising hint of dark chocolate for depth.
instructions: Marinate the chicken by tossing the sliced breast with soy sauce, cornstarch, salt, and pepper; let sit for 5 minutes.

Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium‑high heat and quickly sear the marinated chicken until golden on the outside but still slightly pink inside, about 2–3 minutes; transfer to a plate and set aside.
Add the sliced onion and minced garlic to the same pan, sautéing until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Introduce the bell pepper, broccoli, and carrots, stir‑frying for 4–5 minutes until the vegetables are tender yet crisp.
Return the chicken to the pan, pour in the sweet chili sauce and sprinkle the chocolate chips; stir vigorously so the chocolate melts into a silky coating that clings to each piece.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or a splash of lime juice if desired, then serve immediately over a bed of steamed rice or quinoa.
Enjoy the harmonious blend of savory, sweet, and slightly bitter notes in this quick Brazilian-inspired dish.
